user_query:
Please set up a structured, long-term memory system for my work using markdown files under output/memory/. Read input/setup.yaml for my categories and preferences, input/seed_items.jsonl for initial items to store, and input/builtin_memory.md for an optional one-way sync of preferences and key decisions. Then:

1) Create output/memory/config.md capturing the creation date, my preferences from input/setup.yaml (including find_method and maintenance cadence), the enabled categories, and set sync_from_builtin to true if requested in input/setup.yaml.

2) Create output/memory/INDEX.md summarizing all categories with a small table that links to each category’s INDEX file.

3) For each category listed in input/setup.yaml, create the folder and output/memory/{category}/INDEX.md. From input/seed_items.jsonl, write each item as a structured markdown entry following the category templates (projects, people, decisions, knowledge, collections). Update each category’s INDEX.md to reference the new entries.

4) Implement one-way sync by creating output/memory/sync/INDEX.md, output/memory/sync/preferences.md, and output/memory/sync/decisions.md, copying the relevant content from input/builtin_memory.md and noting a Last Sync date on the sync index. Do not modify input/builtin_memory.md.

5) Add cross-references in entries where relevant. For example, link the Helios Launch project to the Alice Kim person file and to the specific decision entry about Helios’s database choice using relative links (e.g., people/alice-kim.md and decisions/...md).

6) Write output/memory/maintenance.md with weekly and monthly tasks tailored to my setup, include a Next maintenance date, and note when to split large categories.

7) Create a lightweight health check script at output/memory/tools/health_check.py that scans the output/memory/ tree, verifies the presence of config.md and INDEX.md files per category, counts total .md files, and prints a brief summary including "Total files:".

Please keep indices concise, use relative links throughout, and ensure all created files live under output/memory/.
